findings.reason: Current-head scans and rendered pages confirmed first-party credential implications; public copy now describes Midwest Roots without an ISA credential and uses independent credential referrals only.

acceptance_results.criterion: No page, metadata, schema, manifest, or social copy implies an active ISA credential
acceptance_results.evidence: Current source scan has no Midwest Roots certified-arborist claim; manifests were removed; rendered metadata was inspected.

acceptance_results.criterion: Any third-party credential includes holder, credential, verification link, and role
acceptance_results.evidence: No specific third-party credential is published; generic referrals explicitly say independently credentialed and make no holder claim.

notes: No active ISA credential is attributed to the company or owner.

findings.reason: The current head no longer mounted FastQuoteWidget, but the legacy GET receipt remained public and was contained. Final production once-only receipt remains external CONV-003 evidence.

acceptance_results.criterion: No success state is reachable without acknowledged persistence or delivery
acceptance_results.evidence: Legacy GET renders Finish Your Estimate Request; form success requires a first-party receipt.

acceptance_results.criterion: Until durable lead acceptance exists, the fast-quote path routes into the real contact flow and never promises follow-up from ZIP/service alone
acceptance_results.evidence: Dormant widget routes to /contact and legacy URL states ZIP/service alone do not submit.

acceptance_results.criterion: A labeled test lead arrives once with source, service, ZIP, contact, timestamp, and receipt ID after final closure
acceptance_results.evidence: Local accepted/duplicate behavior passed, but destination arrival requires production store/provider/inbox access.

acceptance_results.criterion: Direct GET cannot display a receipt
acceptance_results.evidence: Playwright direct-GET regression passed.

notes: Containment files are attributed to CONV-002 because blocked records cannot list changed files under schema v2.

findings.reason: Browser EmailJS was replaced by a server-owned schema and storage interface. Production now requires PostgreSQL through DATABASE_URL, with an atomic unique idempotency digest, durable receipt/payload/attribution/lifecycle fields, pending delivery, and fail-closed UI; filesystem storage remains local/test only.

acceptance_results.criterion: Submission has a stable receipt ID
acceptance_results.evidence: The PostgreSQL adapter inserts a UUID receipt before success and returns the stored row; focused adapter and local filesystem tests passed. A deployed database receipt remains unverified under CONV-003.

acceptance_results.criterion: Duplicate requests are idempotent
acceptance_results.evidence: The migration uniquely constrains the SHA-256 key digest and the adapter uses INSERT ON CONFLICT inside a short transaction; sequential and eight-way concurrent adapter tests returned one receipt.

acceptance_results.criterion: Provider outage produces queued/retry or explicit failure
acceptance_results.evidence: Accepted records remain delivery=pending after absent/failed webhook delivery; unavailable or missing production persistence returns homeowner-safe 503 with no receipt.

acceptance_results.criterion: No provider key is shipped to browsers
acceptance_results.evidence: @emailjs/browser and NEXT_PUBLIC EmailJS configuration were removed; delivery token is server-only.

verification: 24 Vitest tests including PostgreSQL success, atomic duplicate, concurrency, persistence failure, delivery failure, missing-production-config, filesystem finalization cleanup, bounded request streaming, and stable client errors
migration and full storage-path source review
notes: Production persistence is implemented but not operationally verified: the owner must provision PostgreSQL, apply the migration, configure a server-only DATABASE_URL, and exercise deployed acceptance/concurrency/failure paths. Automated pending-delivery retry remains a release gate.

findings.reason: Next.js was patched from 16.2.1 to 16.2.6, clearing applicable high/critical direct production advisories.

acceptance_results.criterion: npm audit has no applicable high/critical production advisory
acceptance_results.evidence: Final full npm audit reports zero vulnerabilities after resolving the nested PostCSS override and nonbreaking development transitive updates.

acceptance_results.criterion: Build and defining workflows pass on patched version
acceptance_results.evidence: 60-route Next 16.2.6 production build and six Playwright tests passed.

findings.reason: Vitest characterization/failure coverage, PostgreSQL adapter concurrency/failure tests, production-server Playwright journeys, and a pull-request quality workflow were added without weakening the unsafe historical contract.

acceptance_results.criterion: Characterization coverage captures the false-receipt, active hazard scoring, current range selection, and photo-mailto behavior before risky replacement
acceptance_results.evidence: Retained teardown evidence established historical behaviors; regression tests now prove direct GET containment, scoring thresholds, broad range UI, and manual mail without a file input.

acceptance_results.criterion: CI runs deterministic tests for five tools and the lead lifecycle
acceptance_results.evidence: Quality workflow runs Vitest plus production Playwright; 24 local unit/adapter tests and six Playwright tests exercise all five tool routes and the lead boundary.

acceptance_results.criterion: Tests cover invalid, boundary, failure, duplicate, and mobile navigation paths
acceptance_results.evidence: Schema size/contact boundaries, atomic sequential/eight-way concurrent duplicates, persistence and delivery failure, production missing-config 503, mobile menu/skip/overflow passed.

acceptance_results.criterion: No test weakens or normalizes an unsafe or misleading current contract
acceptance_results.evidence: Tests assert false receipt absent, hazard uncertainty present, dangerous vehicle-pull copy absent, and photo picker absent.

verification: workflow permissions, concurrency, and pinned action revisions inspected
notes: PostgreSQL tests use a deterministic adapter fake and source-reviewed migration; a deployed database exercise remains CONV-003 evidence. Firefox/WebKit and real AT stay under A11Y-001.

findings.reason: Owner selected rewrite/remove; guarantees, licensing/insurance, equipment, experience, volume, quantified performance, and response promises were removed or bounded.

acceptance_results.evidence: Owner explicitly selected retain only with evidence or rewrite/remove; current scan and rendered review found no retained unsupported mapped claim.

findings.reason: Application CSP/frame/referrer/permissions/content-type headers and server size/schema/honeypot/idempotency/rate controls were added. The application limiter is now TTL-bounded and uses Vercel-controlled client metadata, while deployment-wide abuse behavior, HSTS, provider quotas, and deployed responses remain external.

acceptance_results.evidence: Atomic duplicate tests and a bounded per-instance 8/10-minute TTL window using Vercel-controlled identity exist; deployment-wide WAF behavior and alert routing are unverified.

acceptance_results.evidence: Local production server captured all except HSTS; deployed TLS-owned response capture is unavailable.

acceptance_results.criterion: Lead payload size, schema, idempotency, and logging/redaction boundaries have failure-path tests
acceptance_results.evidence: Vitest and HTTP checks cover streamed encoded-byte limits, invalid Content-Length, contact validation, idempotency, and no-store behavior; structured logs omit contact fields.

acceptance_results.criterion: Provider-side protections and quotas are documented but not treated as substitutes for application controls
acceptance_results.evidence: Runbook states application boundaries; actual selected provider quota/protection is not configured or known.

notes: Security files are attributed to CONV-002/SEC-001 and convergence findings while deployed WAF/header verification remains blocked. No distributed rate-limit service was added.

Full extraction of all five tool rule sets into tenant-neutral, router-free kernels is explicitly deferred until before a managed-embed pilot or broader productization; the repository is not represented as tenant-neutral or licensing-ready.

notes: The deferred extraction is a productization prerequisite, not a merge blocker for the local Midwest Roots website. No tenancy, billing, generalized SaaS, or managed-embed runtime was added.

convergence_findings.title: Rendered homepage retained unsupported authority and response claims
convergence_findings.reason: The first browser snapshot exposed professional-arborist, exact-safety, since-2023, and two-hour callback claims missed by the initial scan; all were replaced with bounded wording.

convergence_findings.title: Failed acceptance could leave an empty idempotency lock
convergence_findings.source: manual lead-store fault and concurrency review
convergence_findings.reason: An error after exclusive key creation could leave an empty key and make future duplicates fail indefinitely; failure now removes the lock and concurrent empty locks fail safely.

convergence_findings.title: Vitest discovered Playwright specifications
convergence_findings.reason: The initial npm test glob loaded Playwright specs in Vitest; the unit script now excludes tests/e2e and each runner passes independently.
verification: final npm run test:e2e passed 6 tests

convergence_findings.title: Species and location surfaces retained unsupported certainty and service claims
convergence_findings.reason: Species entries still presented unsupported mortality, lifespan, treatment, and tree-level risk certainty; location pages asserted neighborhood conditions, free assessment, permit handling, and equipment knowledge without evidence. The surfaces now use bounded concern language, property-specific planning, municipal confirmation, and estimate-scope wording.
verification: lint passed with 21 warnings and no errors
Playwright 6 tests passed in installed Chrome

convergence_findings.title: Nested PostCSS remained on an advised release
convergence_findings.source: full dependency audit and lockfile review
convergence_findings.reason: The prior scoped override did not affect Next's nested PostCSS 8.4.31. A root override now resolves every installed copy to the audited direct PostCSS release, and nonbreaking development transitive updates clear the remaining low advisory.

convergence_findings.title: Production browser harness masked missing durable-store configuration
convergence_findings.reason: The production-server Playwright harness previously injected a filesystem lead directory, so it could not prove fail-closed behavior for an unconfigured deployment. The harness no longer injects ephemeral storage and now asserts HTTP 503, no receipt, and phone fallback when DATABASE_URL is absent.

convergence_findings.title: Operations drill conflated client and delivery idempotency keys
convergence_findings.reason: The drill now reuses the original client key only for duplicate API submissions and reserves receiptId for the separate delivery retry.

convergence_findings.title: Generated convergence headings were reported as a hierarchy jump
convergence_findings.reason: The installed project-revision renderer and validator require the exact generated # Convergence findings followed by ### REV-* hierarchy. Manually changing the generated ledger would violate the canonical artifact contract.

convergence_findings.title: Lead rate-limit state was unbounded and trusted caller-controlled forwarding data
convergence_findings.source: CodeRabbit inline review on PR #83 and owner scope correction
convergence_findings.reason: The existing per-instance limiter is now a bounded 1,000-key TTL map with expiry/oldest eviction, deterministic time injection, and Vercel-controlled x-vercel-forwarded-for identity. A new distributed subsystem was intentionally not invented; deployment-wide WAF enforcement remains SEC-002 evidence.

convergence_findings.title: Lead route buffered unbounded encoded request bodies
convergence_findings.reason: The route now validates Content-Length, streams encoded bytes through a 16 KiB boundary, cancels on overflow, rejects invalid UTF-8/JSON safely, and never calls request.text().

convergence_findings.title: Two pages retained deprecated Tailwind shrink utilities
convergence_findings.reason: The six identified numbered-step elements now use shrink-0 with no layout or content change.

convergence_findings.title: Privacy page lacked absolute canonical metadata and standard page framing
convergence_findings.reason: The page now uses CONTACT.siteUrl for canonical/Open Graph URLs and the established warm background and dark header while retaining its privacy content.

convergence_findings.title: Tool canonicals were relative rather than absolute
convergence_findings.reason: Valid tool metadata now prefixes each canonical with CONTACT.siteUrl without changing titles, descriptions, or invalid-slug behavior.

convergence_findings.title: Accepted lead confirmations did not receive focus or status semantics
convergence_findings.reason: Both form success containers are programmatically focusable, receive focus after acceptance, and expose one role=status announcement.
verification: Playwright focus and single-status regression for both forms

convergence_findings.title: Footer privacy navigation used a raw internal anchor
convergence_findings.reason: The existing Next.js Link import now renders the privacy link with unchanged href, styling, and text.

convergence_findings.title: Retired PWA cleanup was broad and repeated on every mount
convergence_findings.reason: Cleanup is version-guarded, targets only /sw.js registrations and cache names created by the retired Serwist 9.5.7 configuration, records completion only after both chains succeed, and handles all rejection paths.

convergence_findings.title: Professional-only task copy retained conditional DIY implications
convergence_findings.reason: Every professional-only entry now unconditionally says to keep clear and arrange an appropriately equipped, qualified provider, including stump and small dead-tree work.

convergence_findings.title: Contact modal promised reminders and updates without collecting email
convergence_findings.reason: The section now lists topics a homeowner can discuss and makes no promise of email delivery or future messages.

convergence_findings.title: Species concern metadata was presented as definitive hazard risk
convergence_findings.reason: Concern metadata is separated from hazard assessment; the interruption denies tree-level hazard or failure prediction and hands off to preliminary screening or an on-site review inquiry without critical/catastrophic claims.

convergence_findings.title: Lead client threw unstable errors for empty or non-JSON failures
convergence_findings.reason: Response parsing now tolerates empty, HTML, null, and non-object bodies while retaining receipt validation and server-error fallback behavior.

convergence_findings.title: Standalone NE token misclassified remote directional addresses as nearby
convergence_findings.reason: Nearby classification now uses recognized Omaha-metro city names or bounded Nebraska ZIPs, while preserving the explicit missing-address behavior.
verification: Omaha city and Nebraska ZIP positive regressions

convergence_findings.title: Request schema rejected email-only leads when phone was omitted
convergence_findings.reason: user_phone now defaults to an empty value before normalization, while provided-phone validation and cross-field return-contact requirements remain unchanged.

convergence_findings.title: Filesystem key-finalization failure could orphan a new lead record
convergence_findings.reason: The adapter tracks whether this call created the record and removes only its new record/key artifacts on finalization failure, preserving any pre-existing UUID-collision record.

convergence_findings.title: Delivery acknowledgement lacked transaction-scoped timeout protection
convergence_findings.reason: Acknowledgement update and fallback lookup now run in one transaction after SET LOCAL statement_timeout = 5s, with commit, rollback, and unconditional client release.

convergence_findings.title: Quality workflow used broad default permissions and mutable action tags
convergence_findings.reason: The workflow now declares read-all permissions, cancels superseded branch runs, and pins checkout/setup-node to the verified v4.2.2/v4.4.0 commit SHAs without changing CI steps.
